category_title: Elementary English language learners using gesture to mediate mathematical meaning-making
scenario_title: Running out of physical resources at the whiteboard chart
minimal_context: Liliana is standing at the whiteboard looking at the 'Hands and Fingers' data chart to determine the total number of fingers for different numbers of hands.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the 'Hands and Fingers' chart on the whiteboard. If we have 2 hands, how many fingers is that?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Lifts both hands up, palms facing towards the board at chest level) 10. (Brings palms down a few inches and then back up)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay. What is the next number of hands on the chart?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: 3. (Right hand fingers extending perpendicular to the chart, both hands motionless)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Yes. So for 3 hands, how many fingers are there in total?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: 0. (Brings in all extended 10 fingers and closes both hands)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at the 'Hands and Fingers' chart on the whiteboard. If we have 2 hands, how many fingers is that?

STUDENT: (Lifts both hands up, palms facing towards the board at chest level) 10. (Brings palms down a few inches and then back up)

TUTOR: Okay. What is the next number of hands on the chart?

STUDENT: 3. (Right hand fingers extending perpendicular to the chart, both hands motionless)

TUTOR: Yes. So for 3 hands, how many fingers are there in total?

STUDENT: 0. (Brings in all extended 10 fingers and closes both hands)

task.content: Identifying the next number in the 'hands' column of the chart
task.learning_goal: determine the total number of fingers

context.materials: data chart titled “Hands and Fingers” on the whiteboard
context.social_setting: Mrs. Dee is to her right

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the student has run out of their own physical resources (e.g., only has two hands but needs to represent three) to solve a problem.
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Brings in all extended 10 fingers and closes BHs (answer space was blank)

behavior_patterns.condition: Using both hands to represent the '2 hands' row on the math chart at the whiteboard
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Continues to hold up two hands with palms facing the chart at chest level extended away from body at chest level
behavior_patterns.internal_state: internal cognitive processing of the chart
related_quotes: I have 2 hands, I think there’s 10 fingers.
